Sentence: Defending champions Argentina began their World Cup title defense in emphatic fashion with a convincing victory over Algeria, inspired by a historic hat-trick from Lionel Messi

Playing his 200th international match, Messi scored the first World Cup hat-trick of his career and became the joint-highest goalscorer in World Cup finals history with 16 goals, equaling the record held by Miroslav Klose
The eight-time Ballon d'Or winner opened the scoring in the 17th minute with a brilliant curling effort after receiving a pass from teammate Rodrigo De Paul
Argentina faced strong resistance from Algeria, but Messi doubled the lead shortly after halftime by capitalizing on a mistake from goalkeeper Luca Zidane, son of French legend Zinedine Zidane
He completed his hat-trick in the 76th minute with a powerful strike from the edge of the box, sealing a memorable win

The victory marked Argentina's first-ever opening-match win at a World Cup as defending champions and provided a strong start to their Group J campaign, with upcoming matches against Austria and Jordan
